subject: Computer Networking
title: Load Balancers
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Load Balancers.

hook.text: One server handles 10,000 users. Your app has a million. A load balancer spreads them across 100 servers.

buildup.text: Load balancers sit between clients and servers. Each request goes to the least busy server.

discovery.text: Algorithms like round-robin, least connections, and weighted distribution pick the target.

twist.text: Health checks monitor every server. If one fails, the balancer stops sending traffic to it instantly.

climax.text: F5 hardware handles millions of connections. NGINX and HAProxy do it in software, equally well.

punchline.text: Load balancers spread the work. No single server drowns.

quiz.question: What happens when a load balancer's health check detects a failed server?
options: It stops sending traffic to that server immediately
It reboots the failed server automatically
It notifies the user to try again later
